coreControls.title: RE accountability
coreControls.summary: The Regulated Entity remains accountable for all models, at every lifecycle stage.

coreControls.title: Board-approved MRMF + RMCB
coreControls.summary: A versioned, board-approved model-risk framework; the RMCB approves high-risk models and reviews tiering.

coreControls.title: Enterprise risk + three lines of defence
coreControls.summary: Aggregate model risk against appetite; independent owner / validation / audit lines.

coreControls.title: Risk-based non-offsetting tiering
coreControls.summary: Tier every model on materiality + complexity (+ autonomy); one low factor cannot offset a high one.

coreControls.title: Complete model inventory
coreControls.summary: No model used unless inventoried; required fields and dependencies per model.

coreControls.title: Ten-year retention
coreControls.summary: Retain decommissioned models in the inventory for at least ten years.

coreControls.title: Model documentation
coreControls.summary: Comprehensive documentation for all models, including third-party, retained with the inventory.

coreControls.title: Pre-development discipline
coreControls.summary: Documented rationale/scope; cost-benefit incl. bias/fairness/ethics; data governance.

coreControls.title: Independent validation
coreControls.summary: Independent RE validation of all models pre/post-deploy, on change, periodically; report to RMCB within 3 months.

coreControls.title: Approval authority + exceptions
coreControls.summary: Tier-based approval authority (high → RMCB); time-boxed exceptions with remediation.

coreControls.title: Deployment + ongoing monitoring
coreControls.summary: Replicable/stable deployment; continuous monitoring of all deployed models.

coreControls.title: Change management
coreControls.summary: Controlled change with impact assessment; material change reopens validation + approval.

coreControls.title: Business continuity + decommissioning
coreControls.summary: Per-model fallback (manual/substitute/backup); orderly, notified decommissioning.

coreControls.title: Third-party accountability + validation
coreControls.summary: RE validates bought models anyway; RMCB oversight regardless of tier; due diligence + audit rights.

coreControls.title: AI scope, autonomy, supply chain
coreControls.summary: Define AI scope; autonomy/reliance as a tier driver; third-party AI supply-chain risk.

coreControls.title: Explainability thresholds + behavioural risk
coreControls.summary: Per-model explainability thresholds; adversarial testing; bias, drift, hallucination controls.

coreControls.title: Red-teaming, auto-update, AI documentation
coreControls.summary: Structured challenge/red-teaming; bounded auto-updates; reproducible AI documentation.

coreControls.title: AI deployment security baseline
coreControls.summary: No new vulnerabilities; secure access, interfaces, APIs and integrations.

coreControls.title: Customer-facing AI controls
coreControls.summary: Prompt-injection controls, AI disclosure, human-handoff for customer-facing AI.

coreControls.title: Human oversight + kill switch
coreControls.summary: Human-in-command; override/suspend/deactivate (kill switch); periodic anomaly review.

coreControls.title: Human factors
coreControls.summary: Guard against automation bias/fatigue; competent reviewers; periodic override/near-miss review.

coreControls.title: Consumer protection
coreControls.summary: No consumer-harming models; grievance redressal for consumer-facing models.

frameworkClauses.title: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, documented

Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems
Roles, responsibilities, and lines of authority are documented

Approaches and metrics for measurement are identified

AI system is validated and verified; results documented

AI system security and resilience are evaluated

AI model is explained, validated, and documented for interpretability
Fairness and bias are evaluated and results documented
Approaches for tracking emergent risks are in place
Treatment of documented AI risks is prioritized
Resources are allocated to manage AI risks
Mechanisms are in place to sustain the value of deployed AI
Mechanisms to supersede, disengage, or deactivate AI systems
Post-deployment monitoring plans are implemented
Incidents and errors are communicated to relevant stakeholders

mappings.note: Both fix accountability/authority; RBI makes the RE accountable for outcomes of all models by mandate.
NIST embeds trustworthy-AI in policy; RBI mandates a board-approved MRMF + RMCB approvals.
Three lines of defence ≈ documented roles/authority.
Senior management allocates resources to manage model risk.
Tiering prioritizes risk treatment; RBI adds a strict non-offsetting rule.
Tiering inputs ≈ measurement approaches for risk.
Both require an AI/model inventory; RBI adds a hard "no model unless inventoried" gate + fields.
Documentation/transparency; RBI ties retention to the inventory.
Both document intended purpose/context before build.
RBI cost-benefit explicitly weighs bias/fairness at design time.
Performance evaluation; RBI adds independence + 3-month RMCB report SLA.
Validation/verification documented.
Approval authority/thresholds ≈ documented lines of authority; RBI adds RMCB + time-boxed exceptions.
Continuous post-deployment monitoring of all deployed models.
Change management/maintenance; RBI adds a material-change re-validation trigger.
Sustaining deployed AI value ≈ business-continuity/fallback.
Deactivate/decommission mechanisms.
Third-party risk policy; RBI mandates independent RE validation + audit rights regardless of tier.
Third-party technology risks mapped.
Human oversight/autonomy mapped; RBI makes autonomy a tiering driver.
Explainability/interpretability; RBI sets explicit thresholds + compensating controls.
Fairness/bias evaluation.
Behavioural/safety risk under stressed/edge/adversarial conditions.
Red-teaming ≈ security/resilience evaluation.
Deployment security/resilience baseline.
Prompt-injection/adversarial-input controls on customer-facing interfaces.
Anomalous-usage monitoring on customer-facing AI.
Kill switch ≈ mechanisms to disengage/deactivate; RBI mandates it explicitly.
Human-oversight policy.
Human-factors oversight policy (bias/fatigue/competency).
Incident/near-miss communication and periodic review.
External feedback ≈ grievance redressal; RBI bars consumer-harming models outright.
AI policy ≈ board-approved MRMF; RBI adds RMCB approval of high-risk models.
Roles/responsibilities; RBI makes the RE accountable for outcomes of all models.
Three lines of defence ≈ defined AI roles/responsibilities.
Senior management provides and documents resources for AI risk.
Objectives for responsible development ≈ documented rationale/cost-benefit.
Data-governance / data quality at design time.
Verification & validation; RBI adds validator independence + 3-month RMCB SLA.
Approval authority ≈ defined roles; RBI adds RMCB + time-boxed exceptions.
Controlled deployment.
Operation and monitoring of deployed AI.
Change/operation control; RBI adds a material-change re-validation trigger.
Operation/continuity; RBI mandates per-model fallback + orderly decommissioning.
AI system technical documentation.
System documentation and information for users.
AI system impact assessment ≈ AI scope/impact rating.
V&V incl. behavioural/explainability; RBI sets explicit thresholds.
Event logging ≈ deployment-security evidence.
Responsible use ≈ human oversight; RBI mandates an explicit kill switch.
Responsible-use processes (human factors).
Supplier management; RBI mandates independent RE validation + audit rights.
Customer obligations ≈ consumer protection/grievance redressal.
Sound development process with documented purpose/design.
Controlled implementation/use and change.
Independent validation ≈ conceptual soundness evaluation.
Outcomes analysis/benchmarking within validation.
Continuous ongoing monitoring of deployed models.
Board/senior-management oversight of model risk.
Accountability sits with the institution/board.
Approval authority under governance; RBI adds an explicit RMCB + time-boxed exceptions.
Risk-based model rating; RBI adds a strict non-offsetting rule.
Roles/responsibilities across the three lines.
Independent internal audit as the third line.
Vendor/external model management; RBI adds mandatory independent validation + audit rights.
Comprehensive model documentation.
Model inventory covering all models.
Risk management system ≈ risk-based tiering; EU requires a continuous, documented RMS.
Testing within the risk-management system.
Data and data governance (quality, bias examination).
Technical documentation (Annex IV) ≈ model documentation.
Automatic record-keeping / logging.
Transparency and information to deployers/users.
Human oversight incl. the ability to intervene/stop; RBI mandates a kill switch.
Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity; RBI adds explicit explainability thresholds.
Quality management system ≈ board-approved MRMF.
QMS roles/accountability ≈ three lines of defence.
Deployer obligations ≈ RE accountability for third-party models.
Disclosure of AI interaction on customer-facing systems.
Post-market monitoring ≈ ongoing monitoring of deployed models.
Serious-incident reporting ≈ incident/near-miss review.
Minimise unintentional bias; accuracy.
Justifiable use of data/models, assessed pre-build.
Regular review of data/models for bias (ongoing monitoring).
Ethics considered in the cost-benefit assessment.
Internal accountability sits with senior management/board.
External accountability: enquiry/appeal ≈ grievance redressal.
Proactive disclosure of AI use to customers.
Explanations to data subjects ≈ explainability thresholds.
